title: Establish and Enforce Secure Configuration Settings
description: Establish and enforce security configuration settings for information technology products employed in organizational systems.

checks.name: Security Baseline GPO is Applied
checks.purpose: Verify a security configuration GPO (e.g., Microsoft Security Baseline or CIS benchmark) is applied and in effect on this Windows Client

checks.name: Password Policy Meets Minimum Requirements
checks.purpose: Confirm minimum password length, complexity, history, and age settings meet the documented security baseline

checks.name: Screen Lock and Idle Timeout are Configured
checks.purpose: Verify screen lock activates after the defined idle period and requires authentication to unlock

checks.name: Windows Defender Antivirus is Enabled and Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm Windows Defender Antivirus real-time protection is enabled and configured per the security baseline

checks.name: Windows Firewall is Enabled for All Profiles
checks.purpose: Verify Windows Defender Firewall is enabled for Domain, Private, and Public profiles

checks.name: Security Baseline GPO is Applied
checks.purpose: Verify a security configuration GPO (e.g., Microsoft Security Baseline or STIG) is applied and in effect on this Windows Server

checks.name: Password Policy Meets Minimum Requirements
checks.purpose: Confirm minimum password length, complexity, history, and age settings meet the documented security baseline

checks.name: Screen Lock and Idle Timeout are Configured
checks.purpose: Verify screen lock activates after the defined idle period and requires authentication to unlock

checks.name: Windows Defender or Endpoint Protection is Enabled
checks.purpose: Confirm Windows Defender or an approved endpoint protection solution is active and configured per the security baseline

checks.name: Windows Firewall is Enabled for All Profiles
checks.purpose: Verify Windows Defender Firewall is enabled for Domain, Private, and Public profiles

checks.name: SMB Signing is Enforced
checks.purpose: Confirm SMB signing is required on the server to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks per the security baseline

checks.name: CIS or STIG Hardening Baseline is Applied
checks.purpose: Verify a recognized hardening baseline (CIS Benchmark, DISA STIG, or OSCAP profile) has been applied to this system

checks.name: Password Policy Meets Minimum Requirements
checks.purpose: Confirm PAM password quality settings (minlen, complexity, history) in /etc/security/pwquality.conf meet the security baseline

checks.name: SSH Hardening Settings are Configured
checks.purpose: Verify sshd_config enforces Protocol 2, disables root login, disables empty passwords, and uses approved ciphers and MACs

checks.name: Firewall is Enabled and Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm iptables, nftables, or firewalld is active and enforcing a default-deny inbound policy

checks.name: Core Dump is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify core dumps are disabled via /etc/security/limits.conf or systemd to prevent sensitive data exposure

checks.name: CIS or STIG Hardening Baseline is Applied
checks.purpose: Verify a recognized hardening baseline (CIS Benchmark or OSCAP profile) has been applied to this Debian system

checks.name: Password Policy Meets Minimum Requirements
checks.purpose: Confirm PAM password quality settings in /etc/security/pwquality.conf and /etc/pam.d/ meet the security baseline

checks.name: SSH Hardening Settings are Configured
checks.purpose: Verify sshd_config enforces Protocol 2, disables root login, disables empty passwords, and uses approved ciphers and MACs

checks.name: Firewall is Enabled and Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm ufw, iptables, or nftables is active and enforcing a default-deny inbound policy

checks.name: Core Dump is Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify core dumps are disabled via /etc/security/limits.conf or systemd to prevent sensitive data exposure

remediation: Apply Microsoft Security Baselines or CIS Benchmarks via GPO on Windows. Enforce PAM password policy, SSH hardening, and firewall rules on Linux. Enable Windows Defender and Windows Firewall. Enforce SMB signing on servers. Validate with a compliance scanning tool (OSCAP, Microsoft Baseline Analyzer, or equivalent).
